---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)

source("R/utils.R")
```

```{r}
# path to data folder on sharepoint
sp_data_to_plot_path <-
  csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data")

state_name <- "United States"
state_name_text <- "the United States"

# read in crime data
# srs_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_us.rds")) |>
#   filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)
# 
# srs_by_cat_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_by_cat_us.rds")) |>
#   filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)

srs_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds"))

srs_by_cat_us <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total / 4,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_us <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012) |>
  group_by(year, group) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported
  )

### import state-level file for mapping
### aggregate counts and create rates
### only need 2019 and 2022 (to calculate change between the two)
### keeping values from each year for tooltips
srs_state_to_map <- srs_state |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year %in% c(2019, 2022)) |>
  group_by(state_name, state_abbr, pop_total, year) |>
  summarize(
    n_reported = sum(incidents_reported, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_cleared = sum(incidents_cleared, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_unsolved = sum(incidents_unsolved, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ### calculate rates
  mutate(
    unsolved_rate = n_unsolved / n_reported,
    viol_crime_rate_100k = (n_reported / pop_total) * 100000
  ) |>
  group_by(state_name) |>
  ### currently multiplying rate by 100 instead of using scales::percent
  ### may be better way to format within hc
  ### calculate change over time
  mutate(
    n_reported_2019 = n_reported[year == 2019],
    n_reported_2022 = n_reported[year == 2022],
    n_unsolved_2019 = n_unsolved[year == 2019],
    n_unsolved_2022 = n_unsolved[year == 2022],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2019 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2022 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2022],
    unsolved_rate_2019 = unsolved_rate[year == 2019] * 100,
    unsolved_rate_2022 = unsolved_rate[year == 2022] * 100,
    unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022 = unsolved_rate_2022 - unsolved_rate_2019,
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = ((viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                        2022] - viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019]) / viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                                                                                             2019]
    ) * 100
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ### now drop 2019
  ### and keep population from 2022
  filter(year == 2022)


min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_us |>
  filter(year %in% c(min(year), max(year))) |>
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1]

pct_change <-
  (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]

change_text <- case_when(
  pct_change > 0.005  ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " more"),
  pct_change < -0.005 ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " fewer"),
  TRUE                ~ "nearly the same number of"
)

pct_change_unsolved <-
  min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[1]

change_text_unsolved <- case_when(
  pct_change_unsolved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_unsolved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point higher than"),
  pct_change_unsolved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_unsolved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points higher than"),
  pct_change_unsolved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_unsolved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point lower than"),
  pct_change_unsolved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_unsolved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
)

max_viol_crime_off <- srs_us |>
  filter(year == max(year)) |>
  arrange(desc(incidents_reported)) |>
  mutate(pct = incidents_reported / sum(incidents_reported))
```


```{r}
### import and prep hex map geo

hex <- sf::read_sf("us_states_hexgrid.geojson") |>
  select(state_abb = iso3166_2)

hex_gj <- hex |>
  sf::st_transform(3857) |>
  geojsonsf::sf_geojson() |>
  jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE)
```


# United States data on reported and unsolved violent crime

## Reported violent crime by state

In 2022, 380.7 violent crime incidents per 100,000 residents were reported to police in the United States. Twenty-nine states had violent crime rates lower than the U.S. average and 21 states plus the District of Columbia had higher crime rates.

The national violent crime rate in 2019 (380.8 per 100,000) was nearly the same as the 2022 rate. Between 2019 and 2022, violent crime rates in 32 states and the District of Columbia decreased, while violent crime rates in 18 states increased.

Data regarding crime and unsolved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr). Violent crime includes homicide, aggravated assault, robbery, and rape. Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total population for a given geography. For more information, see the data sources and methods section below.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2022 crime rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5


### choose blues from csgjcr for now
blue1 <- "#d4e9f8"
blue2 <- "#9ed4ef"
blue4 <- "#007392"
blue5 <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_1 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean = as.numeric(comma(viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022,1)),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2022", "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2022, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2022, 1), "<br>"
      )
    )

### highchart 
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_1,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_100k_2022",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0)
      ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, violent crime rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
    ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Violent crime rate, 2022",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#004270",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |> 
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1, blue2, blue4, blue5)),
    labels = list(
      style = list(fontSize = "12px", fontFamily = default_fonts),
      format = "{value:,.0f}"
      )
    ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
    ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
  hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer"),
        accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE))) |>
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()
# |> 
#     hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/exporting.js") |>
#     hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/offline-exporting.js") |>
#     hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/accessibility.js") |>
#     hc_exporting(
#       enabled = TRUE,
#       buttons = list(contextButton = list(menuItems = list("downloadPNG", "printChart"))),
#       accessibility = list(enabled = TRUE)
#     ) |>
#     hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
#     hc_chart(
#       events = list(
#         beforePrint = render_image_print,
#         afterPrint = render_image_remove
#       )
#     )

```


## 2019 to 2022 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

# Assign colors
darkorange  <- "#7B3014"
orange      <- "#D25E2D"
lightorange <- "#EDB799"
white       <- "#FFFFFF"
lightblue   <- "#9ed4ef"
regblue     <- "#007392"
darkblue    <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_2 <- srs_state_to_map |>
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean = as.numeric(comma(
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022, 1
    )),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in violent crime rate: ",
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022_clean,
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2022): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2022, 1),
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2019): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2019, 1)
    )
  )

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_2$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_2,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in violent crime, 2019 to 2022",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#004270",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  ) |>
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Percent change in crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      c(
        darkblue,
        regblue,
        lightblue,
        white,
        lightorange,
        orange,
        darkorange
      )
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}%",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                  fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer"),
        accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE))) |>
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |> 
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/offline-exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/accessibility.js") |>
    hc_exporting(
      enabled = TRUE,
      buttons = list(contextButton = list(menuItems = list("downloadPNG", "printChart"))),
      accessibility = list(enabled = TRUE)
    ) |>
    hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_chart(
      events = list(
        beforePrint = render_image_print,
        afterPrint = render_image_remove
      )
    )


```

:::

## Reported violent crime trends

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
srs_by_cat_us |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    ) |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_setup()
```


```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year) |> 
  mutate(tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide")

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(min = 0) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_2$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_3$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

to_plot_5 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_5$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

to_plot_4 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_4$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer<br>The FBI <a href='https://ucr.fbi.gov/recent-program-updates/new-rape-definition-frequently-asked-questions'  target='_parent'>broadened the definition of rape<a/> for crime reporting in 2013") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup()
```

:::

## Unsolved violent crime by state

In 2022, 63 percent of violent crime incidents reported to police in the United States were not solved. Thirty-five states had unsolved violent crime rates lower than the U.S. average, and 13 states plus the District of Columbia had higher unsolved violent crime rates.

The unsolved violent crime rate in 2019 (57 percent) was 6 percentage points lower than the 2022 rate. Between 2019 and 2022, unsolved violent crime rates in 12 states decreased, while unsolved crime rates in 36 states and the District of Columbia increased.

Illinois and New York did not report enough data on unsolved crime to the FBI to make reliable estimates.

Unsolved rates are the number of reported crimes not cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. For more information, see the data sources and methods section below.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2022 unsolved rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### create object with states to exclude
states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz <- c("Illinois", "New York")

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_3 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz
  # filter(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz) |> 
  mutate(unsolved_rate_2022 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,unsolved_rate_2022)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2022", "</b><br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", comma(unsolved_rate_2022, 1),"%", "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2022, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(n_unsolved_2022, 1)
      ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on unsolved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ))

### highchart 
highchart() |> 
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_3,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "unsolved_rate_2022",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(enabled = TRUE, format = "{point.state_abb}",
                        style = list(fontSize = "13px",
                                     fontFamily = default_fonts,
                                     fontWeight = 700,
                                     textOutline = 0)),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, unsolved rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Unsolved violent crime, 2022",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#004270",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |>   
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage of violent crimes not solved",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(endOnTick = TRUE,
               startOnTick = TRUE,
               stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1,
                                        blue2,
                                        blue4,
                                        blue5)),
               labels = list(format = "{value}%",
                             style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                                          fontFamily = default_fonts))
                             ) |>
  hc_legend(align = "center",
            horizontalAlign = "bottom",
            layout = "horizontal",
            # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
            symbolHeight = 25,
            symbolWidth = 375,
            x = 0,
            y = 0) |> 
     hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
  hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer"),
        accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE))) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |> 
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/offline-exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/accessibility.js") |>
    hc_exporting(
      enabled = TRUE,
      buttons = list(contextButton = list(menuItems = list("downloadPNG", "printChart"))),
      accessibility = list(enabled = TRUE)
    ) |>
    hc_chart(
      events = list(
        beforePrint = render_image_print,
        afterPrint = render_image_remove
      )
    )


```

## 2019 to 2022 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_4 <- srs_state_to_map |>
   ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz
  mutate(unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
     unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022_clean = as.numeric(comma(unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022,1)),
    unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022_clean = sprintf("%+3d", unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022_clean),
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in unsolved rate: ",
      unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022_clean, "<br>",
      "Unsolved rate (2022): ", comma(unsolved_rate_2022, 1), "%", "<br>",
      "Unsolved rate (2019): ", comma(unsolved_rate_2019, 1), "%", "<br>"
    ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on unsolved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ))
  

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_4$unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_4,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent point change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in unsolved violent crime, 2019 to 2022",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#004270",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  )  |> 
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage point change in rate of unsolved violent crimes from 2019 to 2022",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |>
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      c(darkblue, regblue, lightblue, white, lightorange, orange, darkorange)
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                   fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 300,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
  hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer"),
        accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE))) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |> 
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/offline-exporting.js") |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/accessibility.js") |>
    hc_exporting(
      enabled = TRUE,
      buttons = list(contextButton = list(menuItems = list("downloadPNG", "printChart"))),
      accessibility = list(enabled = TRUE)
    ) |>
    hc_chart(
      events = list(
        beforePrint = render_image_print,
        afterPrint = render_image_remove
      )
    )

```

:::

## Unsolved violent crime

```{r}
unsolved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
srs_by_cat_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_unsolved = pct_unsolved * 100,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", percent(pct_unsolved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(incidents_unsolved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Unsolved rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%"))
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_unsolved = pct_unsolved * 100,
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_unsolved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", percent(pct_unsolved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(incidents_unsolved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_unsolved)

to_plot |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Unsolved rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%"))
```

:::

## Violent crime and unsolved crime by state, 2022

```{r}
### create state-level table
srs_state_to_map |> 
    select(
      state_name,
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2022,
      n_reported_2022,
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022,
      unsolved_rate_2022,
      unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022
      ) |> 
   ### recode all unsolved incident data to NA if state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz
  mutate(
    across(unsolved_rate_2022:unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022,
                ~ if_else(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz,
                          NA, .)
           )
    ) |> 
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("state_name" = "asc"),
    columns = list(
      state_name = colDef(name = "State"),
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2022 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents per 100k (2022)"), 
      n_reported_2022 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents Reported (2022)"), 
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = colDef(
        name = "Percent change in violent crime rate (2019 to 2022)",
        format = colFormat(suffix = "%", digits = 0)
        ),
      unsolved_rate_2022 = colDef(
        name = "Unsolved Rate (2022)",
        format = colFormat(suffix = "%", digits = 0)
        ),
      unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022 = colDef(
        name = "Percentage point change in unsolved rate (2019 to 2022)",
        format = colFormat(digits = 0)
        ),
      style = list(
        fontFamily = "Source Sans Pro",
        fontWeight = 600)
      )
    )
```

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources and methodology

Data regarding crime and unsolved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2012 and 2022 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) and the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime and clearances based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2022 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"} and on the FBI's [Crime Data Explorer](https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/#/pages/home){target="_parent"}.

The FBI stopped SRS data collection after data year 2020 and required law enforcement agencies to use NIBRS to report crime statistics to the FBI. This change decreased the number of agencies that submitted their crime data and thus reduced the reliability of state estimates in 2021. In 2022, the FBI allowed agencies who were unable to submit NIBRS data to submit SRS data again. 94 percent of the U.S. population was covered in the 2022 crime data submissions to the FBI, with most states providing close to complete data. However, the population upon which the estimates were derived decreased by over 10 percent in three states: Florida, Hawaii, and Illinois, so conclusions about crime changes in those states should be interpreted with additional caution.

Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}.

Unsolved rates are the number of reported crimes not cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. A reported crime incident is [cleared by exceptional means](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/clearances){target='_parent'} if a law enforcement agency is unable to arrest or formally charge a person with a crime, but the agency has met the following conditions:
    
a) Identified the person who committed the crime
b) Gathered enough evidence to support an arrest, make a charge, and turn over the person to the court for prosecution
c) Identified the suspect’s exact location so that the person could be taken into custody immediately
d) Encountered a circumstance outside the control of law enforcement that prohibits the agency from arresting, charging, and prosecuting the person
    
Examples of exceptional clearances include the death of the person who committed the crime; the victim’s refusal to cooperate with the prosecution after the person who committed the crime has been identified; or the denial of extradition.
:::
